flashcards.front: What is the primary purpose of evaluating individual software projects?
flashcards.back: To determine whether deliverables meet specified objectives, stakeholder expectations, and quality standards; measure project success; identify deviations from baseline plans; and provide feedback for continuous improvement.

flashcards.front: Define Earned Value Management (EVM) in project evaluation.
flashcards.back: A quantitative technique that integrates cost, schedule, and scope measurements to assess project performance and progress. Key metrics include Planned Value (PV), Earned Value (EV), Actual Cost (AC), Schedule Variance (SV=EV-PV), and Cost Variance (CV=EV-AC).

flashcards.front: What is the Cost Performance Index (CPI) formula?
flashcards.back: CPI = Earned Value / Actual Cost (CPI = EV/AC). A CPI < 1 indicates cost overrun; CPI > 1 indicates cost underrun; CPI = 1 indicates on-budget.
SPI = Earned Value / Planned Value (SPI = EV/PV). SPI < 1 indicates schedule delay; SPI > 1 indicates ahead of schedule; SPI = 1 indicates on schedule.

An international standard for software product quality evaluation defining eight quality characteristics: functional suitability, reliability, usability, efficiency, maintainability, portability, security, and compatibility.

flashcards.front: What is a Weighted Scoring Model in project evaluation?
flashcards.back: A quantitative method where evaluation criteria are assigned weights based on importance, scores are assigned to each criterion, and a weighted sum calculates the overall project score: Total Score = Σ(weight_i × score_i).

flashcards.front: Define Return on Investment (ROI) for software projects.
flashcards.back: ROI = (Net Benefits / Total Cost) × 100%, where Net Benefits = Total Benefits - Total Cost. It measures the profitability of a project relative to its investment.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of Post-Implementation Review (PIR)?
flashcards.back: A formal evaluation conducted after project completion to assess whether objectives were achieved, identify lessons learned, document best practices, and provide input for future projects.

flashcards.front: What is the Critical Ratio (CR) in EVM?
flashcards.back: CR = EV/AC × PV/EV = CPI × (1/SPI). It combines schedule and cost performance to indicate overall project health. CR = 1 indicates perfect performance.

flashcards.front: Name the eight quality characteristics in ISO/IEC 25010.
flashcards.back: Functional Suitability, Reliability, Usability, Efficiency, Maintainability, Portability, Security, and Compatibility.

flashcards.front: What is a Project Health Check?
flashcards.back: A periodic evaluation that assesses project status against baseline plans using traffic light indicators (green/yellow/red) for cost, schedule, scope, and quality dimensions.
The projected total cost of the project. EAC = BAC/CPI (assuming current performance continues) or EAC = AC + ETC (Actual Cost + Estimate to Complete).
